pattern: Only the counts of two disjoint letter categories matter, so a single left-to-right tally followed by one integer division settles the answer.
hints: Walk the string once, keeping separate running counts of vowels and consonants; ignore any character that is not a letter.
A letter is a vowel only if it is one of a, e, i, o, u; every other letter counts toward the consonant tally.
Once you have both totals, the answer is the integer (floor) division of the vowel count by the consonant count, or 0 when there are no consonants at all.

edge_inputs.description: no consonants at all across letters, digits and spaces
digits and spaces mixed among letters must not be miscounted as consonants
many vowels versus one consonant, exercising floor division truncation
maximum length string of a single consonant repeated

stress.description: random mix of lowercase letters, digits and spaces at the max length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    pool = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789 '
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(pool) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
